page: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose cloud server built on the x86_64 architecture and hosted on a KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. The CPU configuration consists of 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, supported by 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage along with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The b3-8 achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 10%, and demonstrates strong single-core Geekbench and text processing performance in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core and memory-bound operations show clear resource constraints. The stress-ng multi-core performance and uncached large-block memory read/write speeds fall into the average tier, while Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, Passmark memory, and Passmark database operations rank in the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput and Redis SET operations are also limited, placing in the bottom 25% tier.

This hardware profile represents a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require massive parallelization. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, text processing, and software development environments. However, the 2-vCPU limit and lower multi-core benchmark results present clear performance tradeoffs, making this instance less suitable for heavy database clusters, intensive image processing, or large language model inference.
description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a 2.3 GHz AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core benchmarks placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. However, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities are more limited, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark database operations scoring in the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the b3-8 a cost-effective option for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, text processing, and development environments, while presenting clear performance tradeoffs for highly parallelized or memory-intensive database workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose server featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-8 is a general-purpose server with 2 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core server featuring NVMe storage and strong single-core performance for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with 1 thread per core
8.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
KVM hypervisor with 1 complimentary public IPv4 address
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and development tasks
